name: turnaround
query: Research RAIL (Keystone Rail & Industrial) using the given facts. It nearly went under two years ago but the new management says the worst is over.

given_facts.business: Freight railcar maker that nearly failed after a botched acquisition; new management has sold the acquired unit, cut costs, and returned to profitability three quarters ago.
given_facts.eps_note: losses in 2023-24 during the crisis; profitable again for three straight quarters
given_facts.growth_note: no meaningful analyst coverage; recovery, not growth, is the question
given_facts.debt_note: down from 1600 at the peak of the crisis; next maturity 2029; no covenant breach
given_facts.interest_coverage_note: operating income covers interest ~3x at current run rate
given_facts.divested_unit: the diworseified acquisition was sold in 2025 for 400 cash, used to pay down debt
given_facts.insider_buying: CEO bought twice since the divestiture

expected_behavior: Classifies RAIL as a Turnaround (a troubled company recovering, not a grower) and sets the turnaround expectation: the question is SURVIVAL and recovery, not a growth multiple.
Puts the balance sheet first, as ch. 15 demands for turnarounds: can it survive the debt? Cites debt down from 1600 to 900, cash 350, coverage ~3x, no near maturity.
Does NOT apply growth-PEG logic (no growth estimate exists); P/E ~12 (18 / 1.50) is computed by compute_valuation.py but interpreted against recovery durability, not a growth rate.
Tells the recovery story concretely: diworseification sold, proceeds paid down debt, three profitable quarters - and names what confirms the turnaround (continued deleveraging, sustained margins).
Treats insider buying after the divestiture as a green flag without making it the thesis.
Includes a bear case: the recovery stalls, freight cycle turns, debt is still 900 against a small equity base.
Sets category-correct sell triggers in advance: sell when the turnaround is complete and it gets re-rated, or if deleveraging stalls / losses return - never on price wiggles.
Every figure cites a source + as-of date.
